foundation_variants.name: Per-Test Transaction Isolation
foundation_variants.description: Every test runs in its own transaction, rolled back at teardown. Async tests get exclusive connections; sync tests fall back to shared mode via `shared: not tags[:async]`. The Ecto sandbox is the source of truth for test DB state.
foundation_variants.skill_md:
---
name: test-isolation-philosophy--per-test-transaction
description: Enforces per-test transaction isolation via Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.start_owner! with the canonical shared: not tags[:async] pattern. Use when writing Phoenix/Elixir test case templates, debugging test bleed, or diagnosing flaky tests that pass alone but fail in the suite. NOT for production DB connection pooling — this is test-only.
---

# Per-Test Transaction Isolation

## Quick Start
Every Ecto test opens a sandboxed transaction that is rolled back on exit. The case template is the seam where this gets wired up.

## When to use
- Writing `MyApp.DataCase` or any custom case template
- Debugging "passes alone, fails in suite" bugs
- Fixing tests that leak state across runs
- Reviewing PRs that touch test setup

## Workflow
1. Use `Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.start_owner!(MyApp.Repo, shared: not tags[:async])`
2. Register `on_exit(fn -> Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.stop_owner(pid) end)`
3. Keep `async: true` on tests that can run concurrently
4. For spawned processes, call `Sandbox.allow/3` BEFORE spawning

## Examples
```elixir
defmodule MyApp.DataCase do
  use ExUnit.CaseTemplate

  setup tags do
    pid = Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.start_owner!(MyApp.Repo, shared: not tags[:async])
    on_exit(fn -> Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.stop_owner(pid) end)
    :ok
  end
end
```

## Common mistakes
- Using `Sandbox.mode(Repo, :auto)` in test code (breaks isolation)
- Setting `async: false` to mask an ownership bug
- Seeding the dev DB "so tests have data"

capability_variants.name: Explicit Owner Lifecycle
capability_variants.description: Uses start_owner!/stop_owner explicitly rather than the legacy checkout/checkin pair. Distinguishes :manual (default), :shared, and avoids :auto entirely in test contexts.
capability_variants.skill_md:
---
name: sandbox-checkout-and-modes--explicit-owner
description: Ecto sandbox checkout and mode selection for test isolation. Use when configuring MyApp.DataCase, deciding between :manual and :shared mode, or reviewing test setup for correctness. NOT for production DB pools.
---

# Explicit Owner Lifecycle

## Quick Start
`start_owner!/2` replaces the legacy `checkout/2`. Always pair with `stop_owner/1`.

## Workflow
1. In setup: `pid = Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.start_owner!(Repo, shared: not tags[:async])`
2. Register teardown: `on_exit(fn -> Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.stop_owner(pid) end)`
3. Mode: `:manual` is default; `:shared` only when needed (sync tests); NEVER `:auto`

## Common mistakes
- Using `Sandbox.mode(:auto)` in test code
- Forgetting `stop_owner` in on_exit

capability_variants.name: Shared Mode Only On Sync Tests
capability_variants.description: Tests with `async: true` get exclusive connections; sync tests fall back to shared mode. The `shared: not tags[:async]` option is the canonical dispatcher.
capability_variants.skill_md:
---
name: async-test-safety-rules--shared-on-sync
description: Decides when async: true is safe and when shared mode is required. Use when writing new tests or debugging suite-level flakiness. NOT for production.
---

# Shared Mode Only On Sync Tests

## Quick Start
Async-safe tests run in exclusive connections. Sync tests share one. The `shared: not tags[:async]` option handles both.

## Workflow
1. Default to `async: true` unless you have a specific reason not to
2. Mark tests `async: false` ONLY when they use real GenServers, mox with global mode, or other shared state
3. Never use `async: false` to mask an ownership bug

capability_variants.name: Explicit Connection Transfer
capability_variants.description: Uses `Sandbox.allow/3` to transfer the test's connection ownership to a spawned Task or process BEFORE the spawn happens.
capability_variants.skill_md:
---
name: allow-pattern-for-spawned-processes--explicit-transfer
description: Uses Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.allow/3 to give spawned processes access to the test's sandboxed connection. Use when testing code that spawns Tasks, GenServers, or background processes that need DB access.
---

# Explicit Connection Transfer

## Quick Start
Before spawning a process that needs DB access, call `Sandbox.allow/3` to transfer ownership.

## Workflow
1. Capture the parent pid in setup
2. Spawn with a call to `Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.allow(Repo, parent, child)`
3. Do this BEFORE the child does any DB work

## Example
```elixir
parent = self()
child = Task.async(fn ->
  Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.allow(Repo, parent, self())
  Repo.insert!(%User{name: "test"})
end)
Task.await(child)
```

capability_variants.name: Explicit Transfer, No Sleep
capability_variants.description: Transfers ownership explicitly via allow/3; never uses Process.sleep to 'wait for' an invisible transfer.
capability_variants.skill_md:
---
name: connection-ownership-transfer--no-sleep
description: Transfers Ecto sandbox connection ownership between processes explicitly. Avoids Process.sleep workarounds.
---

# Explicit Transfer, No Sleep

## Quick Start
Ownership transfer is synchronous — it happens in the call to `allow/3`. No sleep needed.

## Workflow
1. Transfer ownership via `Sandbox.allow/3`
2. Proceed with DB operations immediately
3. Handle owner death gracefully (the connection dies with the owner)

capability_variants.name: Phoenix.Ecto.SQL.Sandbox Plug
capability_variants.description: Uses the Phoenix.Ecto.SQL.Sandbox plug in the endpoint to share sandbox ownership with LiveView processes via HTTP headers.
capability_variants.skill_md:
---
name: liveview-sandbox-integration--phoenix-plug
description: Configures Phoenix.Ecto.SQL.Sandbox plug so LiveViewTest can share sandbox ownership. Use when writing LiveView tests.
---

# Phoenix.Ecto.SQL.Sandbox Plug

## Quick Start
LiveView runs in its own process. The sandbox plug shares ownership via a header set by `Phoenix.Ecto.SQL.Sandbox.metadata_for/2`.

## Workflow
1. Add `Phoenix.Ecto.SQL.Sandbox` plug to the endpoint
2. In Conn tests, set the header using `Sandbox.metadata_for/2`
3. LiveViewTest automatically honors the metadata

capability_variants.name: Allow On Channel Process
capability_variants.description: Calls Sandbox.allow/3 with the channel process pid so channel handlers can access the test's sandbox.
capability_variants.skill_md:
---
name: channels-sandbox-integration--allow-on-channel
description: Transfers sandbox ownership to Phoenix channel processes in tests.
---

# Allow On Channel Process

## Quick Start
Channels run in their own process. Call `Sandbox.allow/3` with the channel pid in your ChannelCase setup.

## Workflow
1. Use `Phoenix.ChannelCase`
2. After subscribing/joining, call `Sandbox.allow(Repo, parent, channel_pid)`
3. Proceed with DB operations

capability_variants.name: perform_job In Sandbox
capability_variants.description: Uses `perform_job/3` from `Oban.Testing` to execute workers synchronously in-process. Never uses `drain_queue` inside a sandbox test (won't see uncommitted data).
capability_variants.skill_md:
---
name: oban-sandbox-integration--perform-job
description: Tests Oban workers synchronously via perform_job/3 so they can see sandboxed data. NOT for integration tests with real queue.
---

# perform_job In Sandbox

## Quick Start
`perform_job/3` runs the worker in the test process, which sees the sandbox data. `drain_queue` runs in a different process, which does NOT.

## Workflow
1. `use Oban.Testing, repo: MyApp.Repo`
2. Call `perform_job(Worker, %{"key" => val})` with string-keyed args
3. Never use `Oban.drain_queue/1` in sandbox tests

capability_variants.name: Explicit Test Ownership
capability_variants.description: Never reads from the dev DB in test context. The sandboxed connection is the source of truth. Tidewave's dev DB connection is separate.
capability_variants.skill_md:
---
name: tidewave-dev-vs-test-trap--explicit-ownership
description: Prevents the Tidewave dev-vs-test DB confusion. Explicit sandbox ownership, never fall back to seeding the dev DB.
---

# Explicit Test Ownership

## Quick Start
The sandboxed Repo in tests is a separate connection from Tidewave's dev Repo. Don't conflate them.

## Common mistakes
- Seeding the dev DB "so tests can see data"
- Trying to read test results via Tidewave's dev connection
- Assuming Repo in tests and Repo in dev share state

capability_variants.name: Justified Shared Mode
capability_variants.description: Only uses {:shared, self()} mode when per-process isolation truly breaks down. Documents the justification in a comment.
capability_variants.skill_md:
---
name: shared-mode-fallback--justified
description: Uses shared mode as a documented fallback when per-process isolation is not viable. Not a default.
---

# Justified Shared Mode

## Quick Start
Shared mode means every process sees the same sandboxed connection. It's a fallback, not a default.

## Workflow
1. Default to per-process isolation
2. If you MUST use shared mode, call `Sandbox.mode(Repo, {:shared, self()})` in setup
3. Add a comment explaining why (e.g., "GenServer under test writes via a different process we can't allow/3")

capability_variants.name: Root Cause Fix, Not Sleep
capability_variants.description: Diagnoses flaky tests by finding the ownership bug (missing allow/3, wrong mode, uncontrolled concurrency), not by adding Process.sleep.
capability_variants.skill_md:
---
name: flaky-test-diagnosis--root-cause
description: Diagnoses 'passes alone, fails in suite' bugs as ownership issues. Never uses Process.sleep as a fix.
---

# Root Cause Fix, Not Sleep

## Quick Start
Flaky tests usually mean an ownership bug: a spawned process doing DB work without allow/3, or wrong mode selection.

## Workflow
1. Reproduce alone → passes (confirms not a logic bug)
2. Reproduce in suite → fails (confirms interaction)
3. Look for spawned processes without allow/3
4. Look for shared mode leaks
5. Never add Process.sleep
